title: <x-option>
breadcrumbs: XHTML Elements for Static Server Pages
text: <x-option> Uniface-specific XHTML tag that enables or disables a specific type of substitution tag ( <x-subst> ) for the whole static server page. <x-option SubstitutionType ="true" | "false"> Example: <x-option inclFile="false" /> Arguments SubstitutionType —supported value of the type attribute of a <x-subst> tag. For more information, see <x-subst> . Boolean —Uniface Boolean value, that is, TRUE or FALSE (or their equivalents). Description The <x-option> tag modifies the behavior of webgen , and so affects the entire web page. By default, all types of substitution tags ( <x-subst> ) in the server page are enabled. You can use the <option> tag to disable a specific type of substitution, but this will then be applied to all substitutions of the same type. It is not possible to disable a substitution type at one point in server page and then enable it at a later point. For example, if your server page includes several substitution tags to include files ( <x-subst type="inclFile" name="../js/myjs1.js"> ), setting <x-option inclFile="false"> will mean that none of your files will be included in the resulting HTML. Related Links webgen Generate an XHTML document from the layout definition and the component definition of a static server page. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
